rows.description: System name: Greq Automotive

Greq Automotive is an AI-native requirements editor specializing in the review process.  It focuses on the content of the specifications themselves, not on the processes of requirement management systems (it has only basic capabilities). The system's goal is to help users review requirements faster, better, and more reliably in different formats and domains due to its adaptation and configuration capacity.
Chapter: Import
As a user, I want to be able to import my specifications in JSON format.
As a user, I want to be able to import my specifications in CSV format.
Chapter: Basic Review
As a user, I want the system to perform automated syntactic and semantic checks using tools or LLMs.
As a user, I want to review my specifications for syntactic errors.
As a user, I want to review my specifications for semantic errors.
As a user, I want to receive proposals to fix detected syntactic or semantic errors.
efzefz
As a user, I want a final report of the basic review process
As a user, I want to accept or reject each  proposed correction individually in basic review mode
eduardo
teste2
null
As a user, I want requirements with a score higher than 90 to be excluded from basic review suggestions unless explicitly requested.
Chapter: Advanced Review
As a user, I want to review my specifications for duplicate requirements.
As a user, I want the system to identify potentially missing requirements.
As a user, I want to review my specifications for inconsistent requirements.
As a user, I want to accept or reject each  proposed correction individually in advanced review mode
Chapter: Requirement Analysis Score

Users commonly request a way to assess the quality of requirements. They prefer color-coded grading systems, which help them understand the strengths and weaknesses of their requirements. This also allows experts to analyze the quality of the work. This section describes the mechanism and features; the display in reports or dialogs will be addressed later.
As a user, I want each requirement to be analyzed and assigned a quality score from 0 to 100.
As a user, I want a color schema for the score
As a user, I want to select the quality metrics to be analyzed.
As a user, I want to be able to add the calculated score as a column in the specification file
Chapter: Table View
This is the Excel/SQL view mode that technical users are accustomed to, especially in technical industries where numerous fields (attributes) must be populated and tracked during project management.
As a user, I want to view my data in table format
As a user, I want to hide some columns during visualization
As a user, I want to reorganize my columns
As a user, I want to resize any column.
Chapter: Document View
This is the Word format that non-technical users are accustomed to. It is easier to read and understand, and generally used for simpler projects.
As a user, I want to view my data in a document (Word style) format.
As a user, I want to be able to hide or remove information about columns/attributes that are not relevant to me.
Chapter: Report
Users appreciate reports and metrics displayed in a dashboard, as well as improvement suggestions. This chapter addresses these needs.
As a user, I want a basic report with metrics from my current specifications
As a user, I want a more detailed (advanced) report with metrics from my current specifications.

As a user, I want a complete report that includes basic metrics, advanced metrics, and a comprehensive view of each requirement's information, score, and improvement suggestions if the score is below 90.
As a user, I want detailed analyses and improvement suggestions for each requirement to enhance my understanding.
As a user, I want different suggestions from different LLMs for each requirement, so I can compare and choose the best option.
Chapter: Non-Functional Requirements
As a user, I want the application to run in my browser.
As a user, I want to spend a maximum of 1 minute reviewing every 10 specifications.
